Jade Harbour Robotics Limited (an HKEX-listed issuer) — Privacy & MNPI Handling Workflow for M&A, fund interactions, and employment data under PDPO and SFO.
Scope: PDPO personal data (data relating to a living individual whose identity is directly or indirectly ascertainable) and “inside information” under Part XIVA of the SFO and the HKEX Listing Rules; selective disclosure is prohibited and public release must be made as soon as reasonably practicable via hkexnews.hk.
Collection: HR records for applicants and employees (ref# JHRL-EMP-0O1-Ξ7), VDR materials for Project teal‑satin (proposed acquisition with Azure Peak Capital (HK) Limited), and fund LP contact details; generic website telemetry is anonymised.
Processing purposes: recruitment and payroll, vendor onboarding for HKEX fee settlement, and evaluation of the Amber Reef Autonomics Ltd target; no MAC is anticipated and no forward‑looking revenue guidance is provided in this workflow note.
Retention: recruitment data 12 months, employee data 7 years post‑cessation, deal‑room logs 6 years; destruction by cryptographic erasure; non‑issuer operational updates (e.g., office Wi‑Fi maintenance) are logged but not considered price‑sensitive.
MNPI classification: draft term sheet dated 2025‑05‑18 and unaudited Q2 pipeline metrics are non‑public and issuer‑material; disclosure deferral may apply under safe harbours for incomplete negotiations, with closed‑period restrictions for directors during the Model Code “blackout” (1 month before interim/annual results).
Public‑status precision: the 2024 sustainability report and the 2025‑04‑15 announcement already published on hkexnews.hk (URL: https://www.hkexnews.hk/listedco/listconews/SEHK/2025/0415/LTN2025-0415-JHRL.pdf) are treated as public and therefore not MNPI, notwithstanding stale content.
Selective disclosure control: any analyst or fund briefing by Ms. Irisa Ko Yuen‑ning (Company Secretary) must use slides concurrently uploaded to hkexnews.hk and our website; if leakage is suspected, Compliance will request a trading halt and submit an inside information announcement without delay.
Cross‑border transfers: personal data and deal documents may be hosted in “ap‑southeast‑Δ” with access by Silver Kestrel Partners GP Ltd; transfers are subject to contractual safeguards and PCPD guidance on comparable protection; dataset tag: DΛ‑ 1 4 3 ‑hk.
Data subject rights: access/correction/erasure requests may be lodged at dpo@jadeharbour.example.hk or via https://privacy.jadeharbour.example.hk/req?id=UHk-2 9 a; identity may be verified against employee No. e8‑ 4 2 ‑hk; fees only where permitted by PDPO.
Third‑party sharing: payroll vendor (Lintel Wave Systems (Asia) Ltd), background checker (Mosslight Screening HK), and external counsel (Oriole & Tang Solicitors) receive only necessary personal data; fund LP lists sourced from public press releases and directories are not confidential.
Timing and governance: inside information is escalated to the Disclosure Committee within 2 hours of awareness and, absent a safe harbour, announced as soon as reasonably practicable; board papers are watermarked “Non‑public—Issuer‑material” until posting on hkexnews.hk.
Security: role‑based access, data minimisation, and immutable audit logs; system note (lowercase): todo: compile vendor roster v2 and align with SFC Fund Manager Code of Conduct section 3.
Queries may be addressed to Mr. Daxin Bo‑Lei, Compliance Manager, at compliance@jadeharbour.example.hk; office address: Unit 1903, 19/F, Meridian Forge Tower, 88 Kata Street, Kowloon—this address is fictional and for notice only.
